MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER OF DEFT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF HICKS, DAWN N AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR MAGGIE HICKS DAWN N HICKS AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE MAGGIE LEE HICKS FAMILY TRUST

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS OVERRULED IN PART, AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF PLEADS THE LEGAL EFFECT OF THE CONTRACT AND ANY AMBIGUITIES CAN BE CLARIFIED IN DISCOVERY. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. FOR PURPOSES OF THIS DEMURRER, DUTY SUFFICIENTLY PLED AS TO ACCOUNT HOLDER. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ELDER ABUSE. PLAINTIFF QUALIFIES AS THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE OF HICKS WHO MEETS THE DEFINITION OF "ELDER." DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WITH GREATER PARTICULARITY THE SUBSTANCE OF MISREPRESENTATIONS AND OR SPECIFY THE BROCHURES, ADVERTISEMENTS, AND OTHER DISCLOSURES THAT MADE THOSE REPRESENTATIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Wesley M. Lowe, Esq. of Mannion & Lowe, (415) 733-1050, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Juan C. Araneda, Esq. of Long & Levit LLP (415) 397-2222, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled in part, and sustained in part. Demurrer is overruled as to first cause of action for Breach of Contract. Plaintiff pleads the legal effect of the contract and any ambiguities can be clarified in discovery. Demurrer is overruled as to third cause of action for Negligence. For purposes of this demurrer, duty sufficiently pled as to account holder. Demurrer is overruled as to eighth cause of action for Elder Abuse. Plaintiff qualifies as the legal representative of Hicks who meets the definition of "elder." Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for Negligent Misrepresentation. Plaintiff must allege with greater particularity the substance of misrepresentations and or specify the Brochures, advertisements, and other disclosures that made those representations. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW/DC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
